summary: Filter infrastructure paths (.twining/, node_modules/) from code-level file change metrics and add git checkpoints between agent sessions
context: Phase 0 benchmark results showed full-twining at 2.4M tokens and 63K lines added vs ~1M tokens and ~1K lines for other conditions. Investigation revealed .twining/ files (30K-line tokenizer.json), node_modules/.vite/ cache, and session 2 double-counting session 1 changes were corrupting metrics.
rationale: Three-pronged fix: (1) .gitignore in fixtures prevents infrastructure files from entering git index for new runs, (2) data-collector.enrichAndSave splits computeFileChanges results into fileChanges (code only) and infrastructureFileChanges (infrastructure), (3) commitSessionSnapshot between sessions creates a git checkpoint so each session's diff is isolated. Scorers read transcript.fileChanges and automatically get clean data without any scorer changes.

alternatives.option: Filter at scorer level instead of data-collector
alternatives.reason_rejected: Would require changing every scorer independently, and raw data would still be corrupted. Better to fix at the source.
.gitignore prevents future runs from tracking infra files, but doesn't help if agent modifies .gitignore or if git add --force is used. Belt-and-suspenders approach is safer.
